states.name: Diabolos
states.title: Dragon Lord of Shadow
states.description: His name is Crescent, and he's a very happy dragon. Or at least, he used to be. He doesn't remember all that much. One day, there was nothing but the dark. The next day, there were Luna, and Solas, and the girl with the fluffy white hair. And grapes! He had no reason not to be happy in a world with grapes and good friends. But one day, that stopped being enough. Something inside him, like a shadow on a moonless night, started following him. Whispering to him. There's more than this. More than this. Remember. Crescent doesn't know if he wants to. He thinks, if he does, he might never be happy again.

states.name: Diabolos
states.title: Dragon Lord of Shadow
states.description: Diabolos... The voice in Crescent's mind haunts him with that name. Diabolos. Not Crescent. Diabolos. You are Diabolos. He doesn't want that awful name. It sounds ancient, larger than he is, cursed with history he can't remember. Suddenly, his days with Luna and Solas seem so small, a speck of happiness against a shadow that threatens to consume him. He wants to protect his friends. He wants to stand by their side forever. But if he is Diabolos, and if the shadow inside him one day shows him its secrets... Will they even want to be near him?

states.name: Diabolos
states.title: Dragon Lord of Shadow
states.description:
The Dragon Lord of Shadow, known as Diabolos, never considered himself an ally of humanity. Only an enemy of the Pandemonium.

For centuries, not a soul set foot in the mountains where he made his home. The dragon would unleash his breath of void on any who came too close, plunging his victims in a realm of perfect darkness. Many thought him utterly invincible.

Except the Dragon Lord of Light, who slew him when he marched with the Bergarian Army as the Emperor's mount.

No one saw the fearsome Diabolos after that. Most thought the world rid of him forever, including the other Lords.

But perhaps it wasn't death he found in that ancient final fight. Perhaps it was a second chance.
